tools.name: list_audiences
tools.docstring:
List all audience personas for your project.

Shows persona names, IDs, and brief descriptions. Use get_audience
for full details on a specific persona.

tools.name: get_audience
tools.docstring:
Get full details of a specific audience persona.

Returns demographics, pain points, motivations, content preferences,
and platform behavior for the persona.

Args:
    audience_id: The audience persona ID or name.

tools.name: write_linkedin_post
tools.docstring:
Write a LinkedIn post. Optimized for professional engagement: hook-driven opening, 1300 char limit, relevant hashtags.

Args:
    topic: What the post should be about.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID (from list_audiences).
    tone: Desired tone (e.g., professional, conversational, bold).
    key_points: Specific points to include.
    include_image: Whether to generate an accompanying image.

tools.name: write_blog_article
tools.docstring:
Write a blog article. SEO-optimized long-form content with headings, subheadings, and a clear structure.

Args:
    topic: What the article should be about.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID (from list_audiences).
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Specific points to include.
    include_image: Whether to generate a hero image.

tools.name: write_tweet
tools.docstring:
Write a tweet (X post). Concise, punchy, 280 char limit.

Args:
    topic: What the tweet should be about.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Specific points to include.

tools.name: write_facebook_post
tools.docstring:
Write a Facebook post. Conversational tone, engagement-focused.

Args:
    topic: What the post should be about.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Specific points to include.
    include_image: Whether to generate an accompanying image.

tools.name: write_newsletter
tools.docstring:
Write a newsletter edition. Structured with sections, personal tone, clear CTAs.

Args:
    topic: Newsletter theme or topic.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Specific sections or topics to cover.

tools.name: write_press_release
tools.docstring:
Write a press release. Formal tone, inverted pyramid structure, includes quotes and boilerplate.

Args:
    topic: What the press release announces.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    key_points: Key facts and quotes to include.

tools.name: write_email_campaign
tools.docstring:
Write a marketing email. Subject line, preview text, body, and CTA optimized for conversions.

Args:
    topic: Email campaign topic or offer.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Key selling points.

tools.name: write_instagram_caption
tools.docstring:
Write an Instagram caption. Emoji-friendly, hashtag-rich, with engaging hook and CTA.

Args:
    topic: What the caption is about.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Key messages.

tools.name: write_youtube_description
tools.docstring:
Write a YouTube video description. SEO keywords, timestamps, links section, and subscribe CTA.

Args:
    topic: Video topic.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    key_points: Key timestamps or sections.

tools.name: write_tiktok_script
tools.docstring:
Write a TikTok video script. Hook in first 3 seconds, short punchy format, trending style.

Args:
    topic: Video topic.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Key moments or talking points.

tools.name: write_website_copy
tools.docstring:
Write website copy. Clear value proposition, scannable layout, conversion-oriented.

Args:
    topic: Page or section topic.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Key benefits or features.

tools.name: write_ad_copy
tools.docstring:
Write advertising copy. Headline + body + CTA, A/B test variants.

Args:
    topic: Product, service, or campaign to advertise.
    audience_id: Target audience persona ID.
    tone: Desired tone.
    key_points: Key selling propositions.

tools.name: list_content_plans
tools.docstring:
List all content plans for the active project.

Requires an active project (use set_active_project first).

tools.name: create_content_plan
tools.docstring:
Create a new content plan for the active project.

Args:
    name: Plan name (e.g., "Q1 2026 LinkedIn Strategy").
    description: Brief description of the plan.
    user_input: Additional context or preferences (max 500 chars).
    audience_ids: Target audience IDs to include.

tools.name: get_content_plan
tools.docstring:
Get details of a specific content plan.

Args:
    plan_id: The content plan ID.

tools.name: generate_content_plan
tools.docstring:
Generate content for a content plan using AI.

This fills the plan with scheduled content pieces. Takes 1-3 minutes.

Args:
    plan_id: The content plan ID to generate for.
    start_date: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD).
    end_date: End date (YYYY-MM-DD).
    platforms: Target platforms (e.g., ['linkedin', 'blog']).
    persona_id: Target audience persona ID.

tools.name: get_content_plan_status
tools.docstring:
Check the status of content plan generation.

Args:
    execution_id: The generation execution ID.

tools.name: generate_image
tools.docstring:
Generate an image using AI.

Creates a professional image based on your description,
styled to match your brand.

Args:
    subject: What the image should depict.
    style: Visual style (e.g., professional, minimalist, bold, artistic).
    size: Image dimensions (e.g., 1024x1024, 1024x1792, 1792x1024).
    mood: Desired mood (e.g., warm, energetic, calm, corporate).

tools.name: analyze_content
tools.docstring:
Analyze a piece of content for insights, quality, and key themes.

Args:
    content: The content text to analyze (min 50 characters).
    source_url: Original URL of the content.
    source_type: Type of content (article, social_post, video_transcript).

tools.name: score_relevance
tools.docstring:
Score the relevance of content items to your brand and audience.

Each item should have 'content' (text) and optionally 'title', 'source_url'.

Args:
    items: List of content items to score. Each must have a 'content' key.

tools.name: validate_voice
tools.docstring:
Validate that content matches your brand voice and tone guidelines.

Checks against your content_strategy knowledge for tone compliance.

Args:
    content: The content text to validate.
    platform: Target platform (e.g., linkedin, blog).
    intended_tone: The intended tone of the content.

tools.name: crawl_urls
tools.docstring:
Crawl web URLs and extract content.

Useful for analyzing competitor content, gathering research,
or importing content for analysis.

Args:
    urls: List of URLs to crawl (max 20).
    extract_metadata: Whether to extract page metadata.

tools.name: discover_sources
tools.docstring:
Discover and orchestrate content from multiple sources.

Finds relevant content from RSS feeds, websites, and other sources,
scores them for relevance, and returns the best matches.

Args:
    sources: List of source configs. Each needs 'url' and 'type' (rss, website, api).
    max_items: Maximum items to return.
    score_threshold: Minimum relevance score (0-1).

tools.name: get_source_status
tools.docstring:
Check the status of a source discovery operation.

Args:
    source_id: The source operation ID.

tools.name: get_knowledge
tools.docstring:
Read a knowledge domain from your project's knowledge base.

Available domains:
- company_profile: Company info, website, industry
- market_intelligence: Competitors, trends, market data
- audience_data: Audience personas, segments, preferences
- content_strategy: Strategy, pillars, tone, goals
- historical_outputs: Previously generated content

Args:
    domain: The knowledge domain to read.

tools.name: update_knowledge
tools.docstring:
Update a knowledge domain in your project's knowledge base.

Use this to manually update or correct knowledge that was
auto-generated during onboarding.

Args:
    domain: The knowledge domain to update (e.g., 'company_profile').
    data: The updated knowledge data as a dictionary.

tools.name: search_knowledge
tools.docstring:
Search across knowledge domains.

Performs semantic search across your project's knowledge base.

Args:
    query: Search query text.
    domains: Optional list of domains to search in. If None, searches all.

tools.name: start_onboarding
tools.docstring:
Start onboarding for a new company.

This crawls the company website, analyzes competitors, generates
audience personas, and builds a content strategy. Takes 2-5 minutes.

Args:
    company_name: Company name (required).
    website_url: Company website URL to crawl.
    competitors: Comma-separated competitor names.
    industry: Industry/sector description.
    target_audience_description: Who the company targets.
    platforms: Content platforms (e.g., ['LinkedIn Posts', 'Blog Posts']).

tools.name: get_onboarding_status
tools.docstring:
Check the status of an onboarding process.

Args:
    execution_id: The onboarding execution ID.

tools.name: validate_onboarding
tools.docstring:
Validate onboarding input without executing.

Checks if the provided data is sufficient for onboarding.

Args:
    company_name: Company name.
    website_url: Company website URL.
    competitors: Comma-separated competitor names.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.docstring:
List all projects in your Visibilio organization.

Returns project names, IDs, and statuses. Use set_active_project
to select which project to work with.

tools.name: get_project
tools.docstring:
Get details of a specific project.

Args:
    project_id: The project ID to look up.

tools.name: set_active_project
tools.docstring:
Set the active project for this session.

All subsequent content generation, knowledge queries, and other
operations will be scoped to this project.

Args:
    project_id: The project ID to activate.

tools.name: generate_strategy
tools.docstring:
Generate a content strategy for your project.

Creates content pillars, tone guidelines, and platform-specific
recommendations based on your company profile and market data.

Args:
    platforms: Target platforms (e.g., ['linkedin', 'blog']). Defaults to all.
    goals: Strategic goals (e.g., ['thought_leadership', 'lead_generation']).
    preferred_tone: Overall tone preference (e.g., 'professional', 'casual').

tools.name: generate_personas
tools.docstring:
Generate audience personas for your project.

Creates detailed audience profiles with demographics, pain points,
motivations, and content preferences.

Args:
    num_personas: Number of personas to generate (1-5).
    target_audience: Description of your target audience.
    platform_focus: Platforms to optimize personas for.

tools.name: research_market
tools.docstring:
Research market, competitors, and industry trends.

Analyzes competitors, identifies market opportunities, and
gathers industry intelligence.

Args:
    company_name: Company to research (defaults to project company).
    focus_areas: Areas to focus on (e.g., ['competitors', 'trends', 'opportunities']).
    competitor_names: Specific competitors to analyze.

tools.name: start_workflow
tools.docstring:
Start a Visibilio AI workflow from a natural language request.

The workflow planner analyzes your request, creates a plan with
estimated costs, and (if auto_confirm=False) waits for confirmation
before executing.

Examples:
- "Write 3 LinkedIn posts about AI in healthcare"
- "Research competitors in the fintech space"
- "Generate a content strategy for Q2"

Args:
    request: Natural language description of what you want to do.
    auto_confirm: If True, skip confirmation and execute immediately.

tools.name: confirm_workflow
tools.docstring:
Confirm or reject a workflow plan.

After starting a workflow, if it's in 'awaiting_confirmation' state,
use this to confirm and execute it, or reject it.

Args:
    execution_id: The workflow execution ID from start_workflow.
    confirmed: True to confirm and execute, False to reject.

tools.name: get_workflow_status
tools.docstring:
Check the status of a running or completed workflow.

Args:
    execution_id: The workflow execution ID.

tools.name: cancel_workflow
tools.docstring:
Cancel a running workflow.

Args:
    execution_id: The workflow execution ID.
    reason: Optional reason for cancellation.

tools.name: get_briefing
tools.docstring:
Get a daily briefing for the active project. Wraps BriefingAgent which generates an LLM-powered executive overview plus 3 key developments from the most recent content.

Args:
    briefing_id: Optional briefing ID. If omitted, returns today's briefing.

resources.name: current_organization
resources.docstring: Current organization info — name, ID, subscription tier.

resources.name: company_profile
resources.docstring: Company profile — info, website, industry, products.

resources.name: market_intelligence
resources.docstring: Market intelligence — competitors, trends, market data.

resources.name: content_strategy
resources.docstring: Content strategy — pillars, themes, tone, goals.

resources.name: scoring_profile
resources.docstring: Scoring profile — content scoring weights and quality thresholds for this project.

resources.name: hub_config
resources.docstring: Hub configuration — publishing destinations, channel mappings, and routing rules for the active project.

modules.docstring: Visibilio MCP Server — MCP integration for Visibilio AI content platform.
API key authentication and user context resolution.

methods.name: cached_context
methods.docstring: Return cached context. Raises if not yet resolved.

methods.name: resolve
methods.docstring:
Resolve API key to user context via backend endpoint.

Calls GET /api/v2/auth/resolve-key with the user's API key.
Caches the result for the lifetime of the MCP session.

modules.docstring: HTTP client for Visibilio backend and gateway APIs.

methods.name: _gateway_headers
methods.docstring: Build headers for gateway requests (uses user's vsk_* key).

modules.docstring: Configuration for Visibilio MCP Server.

functions.name: get_settings
functions.docstring: Get or create settings singleton.

modules.docstring: Visibilio MCP Server — entry point.

functions.name: get_session
functions.docstring: Get the current MCP session. Raises if not initialized.

functions.name: run_server
functions.docstring: Start the MCP server with stdio transport.

functions.name: main_sync
functions.docstring: Synchronous entry point for console_scripts.

modules.docstring: MCP session state management.

methods.name: project_id
methods.docstring: Returns active project or default from API key.

methods.name: build_tenant_headers
methods.docstring: Build tenant headers for backend requests.

methods.name: set_active_project
methods.docstring: Set the active project for this session.

modules.docstring: MCP resource modules — each module registers resources on the global mcp server.
Context MCP resources — current org and project info.

functions.name: current_organization
functions.docstring: Current organization info — name, ID, subscription tier.

functions.name: current_project
functions.docstring: Current active project info.

modules.docstring: Knowledge domain MCP resources — expose knowledge base as readable resources.

functions.name: company_profile
functions.docstring: Company profile — info, website, industry, products.

functions.name: market_intelligence
functions.docstring: Market intelligence — competitors, trends, market data.

functions.name: audience_data
functions.docstring: Audience data — personas, segments, preferences.

functions.name: content_strategy
functions.docstring: Content strategy — pillars, themes, tone, goals.

functions.name: historical_outputs
functions.docstring: Historical outputs — previously generated content.

modules.docstring: MCP tool modules — each module registers tools on the global mcp server.
Audience management tools — list and inspect audience personas.

Start a Visibilio AI workflow from a natural language request.

The workflow planner analyzes your request, creates a plan with

Examples:

Args:

    auto_confirm: If True, skip confirmation and execute immediately.

functions.name: confirm_workflow
functions.docstring:
Confirm or reject a workflow plan.

After starting a workflow, if it's in 'awaiting_confirmation' state,
use this to confirm and execute it, or reject it.

Args:
    execution_id: The workflow execution ID from start_workflow.
    confirmed: True to confirm and execute, False to reject.

functions.name: get_workflow_status
functions.docstring:
Check the status of a running or completed workflow.

Args:
    execution_id: The workflow execution ID.

functions.name: cancel_workflow
functions.docstring:
Cancel a running workflow.

Args:
    execution_id: The workflow execution ID.
    reason: Optional reason for cancellation.
